message:
feat(demo_showcase): implement Program options + terminal lifecycle (bd-2tl1)

Configure bubbletea runtime options intentionally from Config:
- Enable focus reporting (FocusMsg/BlurMsg on terminal focus change)
- Enable mouse cell motion tracking when config.mouse is true
- Set window title to "Charmed Control Center" on init
- Bracketed paste enabled by default in bubbletea

All terminal behavior is now driven from Config as single source of truth.
Closes bd-2tl1, unblocks: bd-3ps4, bd-3jg2, bd-1fxl, bd-7iul

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -120,7 +120,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2qx1","title":"demo_showcase page: Logs (styled + filterable)","description":"A live log viewer page that feels like a production-grade log tailer.

Why:
- Logging is where real apps live and die; this is the most convincing place to show `charmed_log` styling + ergonomics.
- It also provides the “ground truth” for cross-page workflows: Jobs should link to logs; errors should explain themselves.

Must showcase:
- `charmed_log`-styled output:
  - level coloring (trace/debug/info/warn/error)
  - structured fields (service, job_id, request_id, duration, user)
  - readable formatting even at narrow widths
- Bubbles viewport:
  - smooth scrolling, jump to top/bottom
  - follow mode (“tail -f”) that pauses when the user scrolls up
  - optional line wrapping toggle
- Filtering/search:
  - filter by level and service
  - query search with match highlight + next/prev navigation
  - correlation filters (e.g., “show logs for selected job”)
- Actions:
  - copy selected range or last N lines
  - export to a file (with a clear artifact path) and show toast confirmation
  - clear/reset view (without breaking follow mode)

Data requirements:
- Log stream is deterministic in test mode and correlated with the Jobs simulation.
- Includes occasional warnings/errors so filtering and drill-down are meaningful.

Acceptance:
- Looks and feels like a real log tailer (usable, not flashy-for-flashy’s-sake).
- Filtering is instant and predictable; follow mode never fights the user.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - filter + search correctness (stable ordering, edge cases)
  - follow mode logic (scroll up pauses, scroll bottom resumes)
  - export/copy behavior produces expected artifacts/messages
- E2E:
  - wizard -> job -> logs correlation scenario
  - logs copy/export/clear scenario (verifies artifacts + JSONL logs)

Notes:
- Avoid “fake rainbow logs”; prioritize legibility and density.
- Ensure `--no-color` / ASCII modes still produce clear output.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:22.489282051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:41:56.936627Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","demo_showcase","logs","page"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qx1","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:22.508975562Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2s8s","title":"SSH mode: basic auth + middleware polish","description":"Optional but high-impact polish: demonstrate `wish` authentication + middleware patterns without adding real complexity.

Why:
- SSH apps are more compelling when they feel "deployable" (even if it’s just a demo).
- Auth + middleware is a signature part of Wish’s architecture.

What to implement (choose one minimal auth mechanism):
- Password auth for demo (env/flag configured), OR
- Public-key allowlist (a small allowlist file / embedded demo key), OR
- "Local only" banner + explicit opt-in flag for public bind

Middleware polish:
- Connection logging middleware:
  - log connect/disconnect, username, remote addr, session duration
- Nice-to-have:
  - idle timeout / keepalive
  - connection banner (app name, version, keybindings hint)

Constraints:
- Must be clearly labeled "demo" and safe by default.
- Must not block non-SSH mode builds (feature-gated behind SSH feature).

Acceptance:
- Demonstrates Wish middleware/auth patterns end-to-end.
- Misconfiguration yields a friendly error/log message (not a panic).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:52.852345698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:27:12.737175397Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ssh","wish"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s8s","depends_on_id":"bd-3chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:52.880077406Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2s8s","depends_on_id":"bd-3xs0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:57.129489814Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2szb","title":"Animations: respect --no-animations / reduce motion","description":"Implement an app-wide toggle to disable motion.

Implementation notes:
- Drive from `demo_showcase::Config.no_animations` + runtime Settings toggle.
- All animations must consult this single source of truth.

Acceptance:
- Disabling motion does not break layout.
- Tests can force no-animations to make rendering deterministic.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:03.384394507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:18.992401579Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","anim","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2szb","depends_on_id":"bd-2fnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:03.424279835Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2szb","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:42.279564351Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2szb","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:01.922600834Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2tl1","title":"demo_showcase core: Program options + terminal lifecycle","description":"Ensure the demo configures bubbletea runtime options intentionally.

Implementation notes:
- Drive behavior from `demo_showcase::Config` (single source of truth), not scattered env reads.

In main/program bootstrap:
- alternate screen on by default (Config override: `--no-alt-screen`)
- focus reporting enabled
- bracketed paste enabled
- mouse enabled by toggle (Config + in-app Settings)
- set window title

Also ensure:
- graceful exit leaves terminal restored
- handle Ctrl+C and QuitMsg cleanly

Acceptance:
- Repeated runs never leave the terminal in a broken raw mode.
- `--no-alt-screen` produces a usable experience and allows demonstrating bubbletea `println/printf`.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:41.147935817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:05.108586734Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tl1","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:39.779293987Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2tl1","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:41.161925295Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2tl1","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:13.369647978Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2ucj","title":"demo_showcase UX: visual design system + theme presets","description":"Define the visual language so the app feels like one product.

Specify:
- Spacing scale (padding/margins), border styles, rounded vs sharp rules.
- Color semantics (primary/success/warn/error/muted/border) mapped to lipgloss ColorSlots.
- Typography/weight usage (bold/faint), emphasis rules.
- Theme presets to ship (e.g., Dracula, Nord, Catppuccin Mocha, Light, ASCII/NoColor) + naming.

Acceptance:
- Concrete rules that can be encoded as shared style helpers and reused everywhere.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:06.814237235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:33:44.210289534Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:33:44.210207271Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss","theme","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ucj","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:02.290972241Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ucj","depends_on_id":"bd-3kcq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:06.837012355Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2xlv","title":"demo_showcase theme: load custom theme from file + contrast checks","description":"Optional but compelling showcase: load a custom theme from disk and validate it.

Why:
- It proves the theming system isn’t hard-coded and can be user-configured.
- Contrast validation demonstrates a mature design approach (readability matters).

Requirements:
- CLI/Config:
  - support `--theme-file <path>` (takes precedence over preset theme)
- File format:
  - pick a single stable format (recommend: TOML or JSON) and document the schema
  - map file values into semantic tokens (not raw ad-hoc styling)
- Validation:
  - validate required fields are present
  - run contrast checks for key pairs (fg/bg, muted/bg, selection/bg)
  - surface warnings in About/Diagnostics and/or as a toast on load
- Failure handling:
  - invalid file should not crash; fall back to a safe preset and show a clear error

Testability:
- Add unit tests for:
  - parsing valid theme files
  - rejecting invalid/missing fields
  - contrast warning behavior

Acceptance:
- Demonstrates lipgloss theme IO + validation in a real workflow.
- The app remains readable and stable even with bad user themes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:27.588370671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:31:46.886871699Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xlv","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:43.964560626Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xlv","depends_on_id":"bd-39zu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:27.601173399Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xlv","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:40.699647818Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2zeq","title":"demo_showcase: quality gates (tests/docs/perf)","description":"Ensure demo_showcase is reliable and maintainable.

Deliverables:
- Tests for core update logic (use bubbletea simulator where it helps).
- A short in-repo run guide + keybindings (README section).
- Performance sanity (no obvious O(n^2) redraws; avoid huge allocations).

Acceptance:
- `cargo test -p demo_showcase` meaningful; `cargo run -p demo_showcase` never leaves terminal broken.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:11.546258289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:11.560105442Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zeq","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:11.560065147Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
